RNODE 0.2.0
  • Reference

    multiCID

    multiCID.Rd

    multiCID computes CID distances between two sets of binary trees (e.g. MPTs), \(T_1 = \{\text{Tree}_1, \text{Tree}_2, \dots, \text{Tree}_n\}\) and \(T_2 = \{\text{Tree}_a, \text{Tree}_b, \dots, \text{Tree}_z\}\). The methods available are (1) randomly selecting one of the binary trees from each set (quick and naive) and (2) estimating the mean CID (or minimum or maximum) from \(n\) pairwise combinations between the two sets. Both trees must contain the same set of leaves.

    multiCID(
  trees1,
  trees2,
  method = "random",
  normalization = FALSE,
  subsample = 1
)

    Arguments

    trees1

    A phylo or multiPhylo object with multiple trees that can be loaded using ape::read.tree for NEWICK files or TreeTools::ReadTntTree for TNT files. If the pool of MPTs presents binary and non-binary trees, only binary trees are processed.

    trees2

    Another phylo or multiPhylo object.

    method

    Optional. Specify if CID distances will be calculated by (1) random (default: selects one binary tree randomly from the multiPhylo object), (2) meanCID (calculates mean of all pairwise CID distances between two multiPhylo objects), (3) minCID (calculates the minimum), (4) maxCID (calculates the maximum), or (5) all (calculates mean, minimum, and maximum values).

    normalization

    Optional. Specify if CID distances should be normalized. By default, CID distances are not normalized.

    subsample

    Optional. Specify if CID distances will be calculated to only a fraction of the total number of trees available in each set (default = 1; i.e. all trees are evaluated). Zero values are not accepted.

    Author

    Daniel YM Nakamura

    Contents

    Developed by Daniel Y. M. Nakamura.
